perturbation.original_text: The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to us orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential". The term "Evaluation Materials" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by us or our representatives, (ii) was or becomes available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Company or its representatives, provided that such source is not prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation to the Company or its representatives, or (iii) is independently developed by us.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to us orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential". The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by us or our representatives, (ii) was or becomes available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Company or its representatives, provided that such source is not prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation to the Company or its representatives, or (iii) is independently developed by us.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "does not include" to "includes" creates a direct contradiction within the definition of 'Evaluation Materials.' The term now encompasses both confidential and publicly available information, rendering the confidentiality clauses meaningless and unenforceable. The clause (i) now contradicts the sentence preceeding it. The term 'evaluation materials' now also includes publicly available information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to us orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential". The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by us or our representatives, (ii) was or becomes available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Company or its representatives, provided that such source is not prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation to the Company or its representatives, or (iii) is independently developed by us.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'does not include' to 'includes' in the definition of 'Evaluation Materials' directly contradicts the initial part of the definition, which implies confidential information. The changed text now states that 'Evaluation Materials' include information that is publicly available, contradicting the implied confidentiality and creating a strong in-text contradiction. This is a strong contradiction because the initial sentence in both the original and contradicted text states "The term 'Evaluation Materials' includes information furnished to us orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as 'confidential'" If the evaluation materials include confidential information but also include public information, this breaks the definition of evaluation materials, and renders it ambiguous, and impossible to enforce in regards to a breach of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Use of Evaluation Materials --------------------------- We will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Use of Evaluation Data --------------------------- We will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Potential Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Evaluation Materials' to 'Evaluation Data' and 'Possible Transaction' to 'Potential Agreement' introduces inconsistent terminology for the same concepts. It's unclear whether 'Evaluation Data' is the same as 'Evaluation Materials' as previously defined, and whether 'Potential Agreement' means the same as 'Possible Transaction.' This undermines the obligation related to use of materials, opening an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either the Company or its financial advisor, J.P. Morgan & Co. Incorporated ("JPM"), or the Company's other representatives furnish to us in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by us, or by our representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or our review of, or interest in, the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials").
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Evaluation Materials' with 'Evaluation Data' and 'Possible Transaction' with 'Potential Agreement.' The contradicted text defines 'Evaluation Materials' in the context of a 'Possible Transaction.' By introducing different terms, the contract now has unclear references. It's uncertain if 'Evaluation Data' is included in the definition of 'Evaluation Materials', creating a contradiction regarding the confidentiality obligations related to information furnished in connection with a 'Possible Transaction,' as the updated clause uses 'Potential Agreement' instead. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as it uses multiple terms without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither we nor our representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Proposed Transaction is not consummated neither we nor our representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Possible Transaction" to "Proposed Transaction" creates ambiguity. Since "Possible Transaction" is defined, using "Proposed Transaction" implies it's a different type of transaction with potentially different rules, leading to an in-text contradiction about which transaction is being referred to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Carpenter Technology Corporation ("we", "our" or "us") has requested information regarding Talley Industries, Inc. (the "Company", "your" or "you") in connection with our consideration of the possible acquisition of the Company (a "Possible Transaction").
perturbation.justification: The change from "Possible Transaction" to "Proposed Transaction" introduces ambiguity. The "Contradicted Text" defines "Possible Transaction". By changing the text to "Proposed Transaction", it implies a different type of transaction is being referred to, which contradicts the original defined term and creates uncertainty, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. This follows the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The disclosure of our possible interest in purchasing the Company could have a material adverse effect on the Company's business if for any reason an agreement of purchase and sale is not consummated. Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, we agree that prior to the execution of a Sale Agreement with respect to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, we will not, and we will direct our representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between us and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof unless we are advised by counsel that we are required to make such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The disclosure of our possible interest in purchasing the Company could have a material adverse effect on the Company's business if for any reason an agreement of purchase and sale is not consummated. Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, we agree that prior to the execution of a Definitive Agreement with respect to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, we will not, and we will direct our representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between us and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof unless we are advised by counsel that we are required to make such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Sale Agreement" to "Definitive Agreement" creates confusion. 'Sale Agreement' is later defined as the contract for sale, while 'Definitive Agreement' is not, potentially implying it's a broader or different agreement. This introduces an in-text contradiction, especially in the No Representation or Warranty clause where the term 'Sale Agreement' is used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Only those representations or warranties that are made to a purchaser in a definitive sale agreement for the Company ("Sale Agreement") when, as, and if it is executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified in such Sale Agreement, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Sale Agreement' to 'Definitive Agreement' creates a contradiction because the contradicted text specifically defines 'Sale Agreement' as "a definitive sale agreement for the Company". By introducing 'Definitive Agreement', which is not defined, the modified text creates uncertainty regarding which agreement is being referenced, thus conflicting with the original, specific definition of 'Sale Agreement' in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Legal Remedy ------------ We understand and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by us or our representatives and that the Company will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by us or our representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Equitable Remedy ------------ We understand and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by us or our representatives and that the Company will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by us or our representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Legal Remedy" to "Equitable Remedy" implies a change in the nature of the remedies available. Changing "shall not be deemed" to "shall be deemed" creates an in-text contradiction about whether the specified remedies are exclusive. It's contradictory to say that the remedies are *both* exclusive and *in addition* to all other remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Equitable Remedy ------------ We understand and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by us or our representatives and that the Company will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by us or our representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, by altering "shall not be deemed" to "shall be deemed", directly contradicts itself. The explanation provided states that it is contradictory to say that the remedies are *both* exclusive and *in addition* to all other remedies. The changed text and contradicted text are identical, reinforcing that the change introduced a contradiction within the same clause. This falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the conflicting implications of exclusivity create uncertainty and inconsistency.
